diagrams.description: Physical Geography is the scientific study of the natural processes and patterns of the Earth's physical environment. This flowchart outlines its framework: the goal is to understand Earth's physical systems and their interactions with human activities. It integrates core branches—geomorphology (landforms and their evolution), climatology (climate and weather patterns), biogeography (distribution of organisms), hydrology (water systems), and soil geography. These are investigated through diverse methods: field observation, remote sensing, GIS, and numerical modeling. Theoretical foundations draw from systems theory, earth system science, and process‑response models. The synthesis enables the analysis of natural hazards, climate change impacts, and resource management, ultimately informing environmental planning and hazard mitigation.
